title: Thermolabile Proteinase K Typical Reaction Protocol
author: New England Biolabs
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bg9wjz7e

steps.title: Reactions may be scaled-up linearly to...
steps.instruction: Reactions may be scaled-up linearly to accommodate larger amounts of substrate and larger reaction volumes. Optimal buffering reagents, enzyme quantity, incubation temperatures and times may vary for particular substrates. Typical restriction enzyme cleanup conditions are as follows: To a 50 µL, restriction enzyme digest containing 1 µg, DNA and 10 units of a restriction enzyme:

steps.title: Add 1 µL, Thermolabile Proteinase K , mix gently
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL, Thermolabile Proteinase K , mix gently.

steps.title: Incubate reaction at 55 °C for...
steps.instruction: Incubate reaction at 55 °C for 00:10:00 to inactivate Thermolabile Proteinase K . Note: Optimal reaction buffers, reaction temperatures as well as additional reaction properties of Thermolabile Proteinase K can be found in detail in the FAQ . Optimization may be required for other cleanup applications (ligation, extension, PCR, etc.)
